topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

My phone buzzed with a notification that made my stomach clench. A profile picture loaded onto the screen—it was him. Mark. Mark Peterson. The name hit me like a physical blow. My breath hitched, and my fingers froze over the screen. It was impossible. He was the reason for so many of the bad memories I tried to bury under layers of routine. A friend request. My heart began a frantic, uneven rhythm against my ribs. I stared at the little blue button, my thumb hovering, trembling slightly. What if he sent something? What if this was some elaborate setup? A cold wave washed over me, making the air feel thin. I quickly minimized the notification, shoving my phone face down on the desk, trying to force my breathing to slow down.

[story 2]

The chime of the incoming request ripped through the quiet of the late afternoon. I looked up, my gaze snagging on the familiar, mocking avatar. It was him. The sight of it sent an immediate chill down my spine, a deep, unsettling cold that settled in my chest. I felt a sudden urge to bolt from my chair, to disappear into the nearest shadow. My hands started to sweat, slick against the cool plastic of my keyboard. Every instinct screamed at me to ignore it, to delete the notification instantly, but my eyes kept drifting back to the screen. A knot tightened painfully in my throat. I gripped the edge of the desk until my knuckles turned white, trying to anchor myself against the rising tide of apprehension washing over me.

[story 3]

It was an unexpected ping, interrupting a perfectly mundane evening. When I saw the profile picture—that smirk, that familiar set of eyes—a profound sense of alarm flooded my system. My vision seemed to narrow slightly. I instinctively pulled my sweater tighter around myself, a purely reflexive gesture. A hollow feeling expanded in my chest, making it hard to draw a full breath. I hesitated, my mind racing through possibilities, every scenario sounding increasingly unwelcome and fraught with potential danger. I let the notification sit there, unread, for what felt like an eternity, my entire body coiled tight, waiting for the inevitable next move.
